# JobID

> JobID makes work computable — the open data standard for skills, tasks and context. It is a bottom-up engine that translates every job, CV, training and search into one shared data model, enabling exact, explainable matching that never breaks into noise. Tagline: "They guess. We compute."

JobID is a B2C2B job-discovery platform and a B2B data/SaaS layer for job matching and workforce optimization. Unlike incumbents (Indeed, LinkedIn, Google for Jobs) and other semantic-matching tools (e.g. Eightfold), which work top-down — taking a job, decomposing it with AI, then guessing similarity — JobID works bottom-up: a purpose-built ontology of skills, tasks and context is the ground truth, and every document is translated into it. The result is deterministic, auditable matching, millisecond retrieval, and results that never scatter into irrelevant noise.

## What JobID is
- Job discovery that understands what a person can do, not the job titles they must guess.
- "The search is the profile": a candidate explores roles, likes tasks, excludes others, and thereby builds a living, computable profile (their JobID). The right work then finds them.
- A shared data model in which job ads, CVs, trainings and searches are all the same object — enabling cross-document comparison with a transparent match score and the exact reasons for each match.
- The same engine powers enterprise workforce optimization: internal mobility, reskilling, succession — matching employees to new roles and trainings.

## Why it matters
- Skills mismatch costs around 6% of GDP and up to $15 trillion by 2030 (BCG).
- AI needs structured, computable work data that does not yet exist; JobID is building that open standard.
- The market: ~$870B today, ~$1.7T by 2030, with no infrastructure player yet.

## Key facts
- Company: JobID GmbH, Eilenau 21, 22087 Hamburg, Germany
- Founders: Michael von Hirschfeld (product & strategy), Henning Haeske (tech, data & AI)
- Status: 4 years of R&D, working prototype, Letter of Intent from Siemens
- Audience for this site: investors (venture capital, tech investors, larger job-board operators)
- Contact: mvh@jobid.com
